programme.desc: In the third week of the competition, the candidates join Lord Sugar at London's Cambridge Theatre, where they find out the next task will challenge them to design a toy for six-to-eight-year-olds, along with an online video to promote their brand. The teams must create their toys and prepare their pitches, before presenting their concepts to some of the biggest names in the toy industry. Back in the boardroom, one candidate from the losing team will be sent home(n)

programme.title: Breaking Fashion
programme.desc: Actress Emily Atack shoots her new summer range for In The Style, and senior creative Hollie oversees the shoot in Adam's absence. During the shoot, Emily reads some hurtful comments about her body in an online newspaper, which knocks her confidence. When Adam sees the images, he decides they should re-shoot the entire campaign(n)

programme.title: The Truth About
programme.desc: Doctor Javid Abdelmoneim explores the science behind drinking alcohol, including why the weekly recommended intake was lowered in January, with the men's limit cut by a third to 14 units per week. He also asks whether a nightcap can aid sleep, why some people get drunk quicker than others, what is behind the relatively healthy reputation of red wine and whether lining your stomach before drinking really lessens the effects of a night out. Plus, he assesses new evidence for the health risks posed by alcohol(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Naga Munchetty and Charlie Stayt present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Live
programme.desc: A look at the spiralling cost of buy-now, pay-later finance deals, and a report on who is at fault when contaminated fuel from a petrol station causes damage to a car(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell and Martin Roberts follow the progress of terraced houses in Frizington in Cumbria and Wolverhampton in the Midlands and a huge commercial building in Stoke-on-Trent(n)

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: A prisoner claiming for injury behind bars forgets that the camera never lies and a bodybuilder is floored after flaunting his workouts when he is supposed to be off sick(n)

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: How a pair of fake registration plates on a getaway car helped a detective to solve a series of robberies. Plus, footage of a supermarket cashier standing up to armed robbers(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: The reds and blue teams go shopping in the antique centres of Hungerford, Berkshire, joined by experts Caroline Hawley and David Harper. Eric Knowles presents and assesses their purchases(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Imogen is running an art therapy class and has to delve deep to get the participants to talk. Ruhma drags Becky back to the Icon Bar, while Karen gets stressed about her project(n)

programme.title: Head Hunters
programme.desc: Quiz show hosted by Rob Beckett, in which contestants choose between working together to combine their knowledge or going it alone to claim the prize for themselves(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Perfect Town
programme.desc: Steve Brown heads to Bideford in Devon to show his buyers a few properties for their £435,000, while also taking in community life, from its busy arts scene to its luxury ice-cream(n)

programme.title: Make Me a Dealer
programme.desc: Paul Martin is in Liverpool, teaching shop manager Dean Brittles and mosaic artist Freya Levy the tricks of the antiques-buying trade to see who can make the most profit(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore rescues a sideboard, a chaise longue and an overcoat bound for the tip, before she and two others restyle them in the hope of making a profit for the owners(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Martin misses Bex's mental health appointment when a pick-up with Tubbs spirals out of control. Stuart blames Kathy for Rainie's predicament, Kush realises how much the Slaters are struggling and Whitney agrees that Tiffany can meet Leo - but is confused when it turns out her sister already knows him(n)

programme.title: Watchdog
programme.desc: Matt Allwright, Steph McGovern and Nikki Fox present the consumer investigation show, including a confrontation with a tech repair business that instead of fixing faults creates new ones - and even holds customers' gadgets hostage. Anneka Rice investigates whether carbon offsetting can really mean guilt-free-flying, and the team ranks the best or worst airlines(n)

programme.title: The Met: Policing London
programme.desc: The observational documentary about the Metropolitan Police continues with the ongoing problem of moped crime. A young woman is rushed to hospital after being violently mugged by two moped-riding robbers who took her phone and handbag. The detectives get a lead just 15 minutes later when two men use the victim's bank card. But tracking them proves more difficult. The film also follows the Met officers keeping crowds safe at the Notting Hill Carnival, at a time when knife crime in the capital has become a major concern(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate from Leicester, inviting the studio audience to question a panel of politicians and other guests on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week(n)

programme.title: Brexitcast
programme.desc: The TV version of the popular radio show taking a light-hearted view of all things related to Brexit and beyond, with Laura Kuenssberg, Katya Adler, Adam Fleming and Chris Mason engaging in banter about the ongoing process to leave the EU(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Naga Munchetty and Charlie Stayt present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Live
programme.desc: With serious failings recently exposed at one of the organisations meant to protect people from fraud, Julia Somerville, Angela Rippon and Gloria Hunniford ask where people should turn if they fall prey to such conmen. Plus, the cost of watching television in hospital, and whether excessive profiteering is going on(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts is in Croydon, south London, at a basement flat bought by two brothers. Martel Maxwell visits a colourful terrace in Cumbria, while Dion Dublin is in Greater Manchester, where a first-time developer has taken on a real challenge(n)

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: A driver claiming for disability with serious back injuries is shamed by surveillance footage that puts the brakes on her claim, while a strange case of a missing phone turns one dodgy claimant into a super sleuth. Presented by Naga Munchetty(n)

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood reveals how the police and the public are using CCTV evidence and technology to catch criminals in the act. Here, a shop owner, recently burgled, is convinced she knows the thief's identity - but now she has to prove it. Plus, RSPCA investigators are called upon to assist when pet rabbits are seen being thrown into a hedge(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning hosts, as experts Thomas Forrester and Stephanie Connell help two teams find items of value in Nottingham. Anita also goes on a personal journey to see the works of Dame Laura Knight, one of her favourite artists(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: It's a big day for Imogen in more ways than one, and Valerie is in for shock when she goes to lunch. Jimmi gets embroiled in a feud as the grand finale of art week is prepared to be unveiled(n)

programme.title: Head Hunters
programme.desc: Quiz show hosted by Rob Beckett, in which contestants choose between working together to combine their knowledge or going it alone to claim the prize for themselves(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Perfect Town
programme.desc: Sonali Shah explores Arundel in West Sussex, in her quest for the ideal location as her buyers seek the right place to spend their £900,000 on. She hands over the keys to some surprising houses, but will her self-confessed 'instant buyers' decide to seal the deal on any of them?(n)

programme.title: Make Me a Dealer
programme.desc: Paul Martin takes on raw recruits and sets them on the road to being successful antiques dealers. Here, Dorset mother-of-two Lucia Foster-Found takes on chef Alex Koerber. Which of the two will triumph at the nail-biting boardroom finale?(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: EJ Osborne use his salvaging expertise at Llanfoist Recycling Centre in Monmouthshire, searching for three items that he can rescue and revamp. A collection of trampoline poles are worked on by carpenter Ollie Allen, while lighting specialist Guy Trench has to come up with a bright idea for a paraffin can and a pair of blow torches. For his own project, EJ has a large desk to keep him busy(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Angellica Bell and Marvin Humes present the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Olympic gold medallists in cycling and boxing respectively Joanna Rowsell Shand and Luke Campbell, former Great Britain sprinter John Regis and Scotland rugby union star Tommy Seymour join team captains Phil Tufnell and Matt Dawson and host Sue Barker on the quiz(n)

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: David Jason returns as Granville in this follow-up to Roy Clarke's popular comedy Open All Hours, as he re-opens the doors of the nation's favourite corner shop. In the opening episode, Arkwright's protege finds an old mangle in the yard - and wonders how he can best put it to use. Meanwhile, Beth continues to pressure Leroy to find a date for her best friend, Ruby, who has been suspended at work. Elsewhere, Eric and Cyril tutor Mr Newbold in the art of romance - and how to get closer to Mrs Rossi. Co-starring James Baxter, Johnny Vegas and Katie Redford(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Sonia is alarmed when she sees Martin's injuries and when he refuses to seek medical help, she grows increasingly agitated with his lack of consideration for Bex. Stuart visits Kathy and makes his feelings plain, while Whitney is adamant that she and Leo stay in the E20 when Callum and Ben walk in - but she proceeds to get drunk. Gray suggests the Taylors throw a party to celebrate Chantelle's pregnancy(n)

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell hosts the satirical quiz, as guests including comedian Ross Noble joining regular team leaders Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's events(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Comedy panel show in which team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack are joined by guest panellists Gabby Logan, Chris McCausland, Angela Scanlon and John Simm, hoping to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves. Rob Brydon hosts(n)

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Original Terminator stars Arnold Schwarzenegger and Linda Hamilton join the host to discuss their being reunited in the movie's latest instalment, Terminator: Dark Fate. Plus, iconic lead singer of Blondie, Debbie Harry talks about her autobiography, Face It, and Nadiya Hussain discusses her frank and honest new memoir, Finding My Voice. With music by DJ Spoony's Garage Classical featuring the Sugababes(n)

programme.title: Fleabag
programme.desc: Claire organises a surprise birthday party for herself. Fleabag helps her 'inappropriate' brother-in-law to shop for the perfect present, and runs into an old friend while trying to find a date. Award-winning comedy, written by and starring Phoebe Waller-Bridge(n)

programme.title: The Apprentice

The teams must create their toys and prepare their pitches, before presenting their concepts to some of the biggest names in the toy industry. Back in the boardroom, one candidate from the losing team will be sent home(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Experts transform and then sell three things about to be dumped at a tip, before giving the profits to the people who were getting rid of the items(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Perfect Town
programme.desc: Presenter Steve Brown helps a bachelor find an ideal property in Guildford, Surrey, on a budget of £420,000(n)

programme.title: Shop Well for Less?

To shock the family into making some big changes, Steph and Alex build a surprise pop-up shop in the family's house with some of their biggest spends, but how will they react when they know the true cost of their big brand spending?(n)

programme.title: The Boss
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Susan Calman in which one contestant becomes the Boss and has to use judgement and tactics to decide which player is best suited to answer questions(n)

programme.title: My Life on a Plate
programme.desc: Brian Turner accompanies Lesley Garrett on a trip to key locations from her childhood in Yorkshire, where she was taught by her parents to make the most out of what was available. The chef then creates two dishes that weave nostalgia and the taste of home together(n)

programme.title: Emergency Rescue Down Under
programme.desc: A former Royal Marine fights fires from a helicopter and a UK flying doctor is called to a major accident in a fairground(n)

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: The search for relatives of an East London lady reveals a family connection to the most famous heist of the 20th century, and one heir hunter takes on much larger firms in Cheshire(n)

programme.title: The Wonder of Animals
programme.desc: Chris Packham explores how the brain of the great ape has evolved, revealing that different areas have been adapted over time by the animal's anatomy, ingenuity and sociability. He also examines how the ability to use two hands asymmetrically sets the great apes apart from other tool-using animals, and how social living is linked to the evolution of the amygdala in both apes and humans(n)

programme.title: Yellowstone
programme.desc: The arrival of summer in Yellowstone brings a new challenge for the beavers as their river dries up, forcing the colony to move. As food becomes scarce, the wolves have a surprising strategy to keep their pups fed, while grizzly bears pay an unexpected visit to a cowboy ranch. By midsummer, the hot and dry conditions create a new danger as deadly wildfires threaten to engulf a family of great grey owls. Presented by Kate Humble and Patrick Aryee(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Experts Will Axon and Christina Trevanion assess items at Ragley Hall in Warwickshire, a 17th-century Palladian house owned by the Marquess of Hertford. The pair unearth treasures to take off to auction including a Polynesian paddle and an 18th-century drinking glass. Presenter Paul Martin also explores the work of famous sculptor and wood carver Grinling Gibbons(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Angellica Bell, Gyles Brandreth, Scarlett Moffatt and Iain Stirling test their general knowledge skills in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games this week(n)

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Zoe Ball presents more backstage gossip and exclusive news from Strictly Come Dancing. Plus, Ian Waite casts his eye over the couples' latest training footage(n)

programme.title: The Andrew Neil Show
programme.desc: Andrew Neil and guests with interviews, discussion and analysis from Westminster, including the latest news on Brexit(n)

programme.title: The Channel Tunnel - Life on the Inside
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes of the structure in its 25th anniversary year, following the teams who keep everything on track through the undersea tunnel. Workers in the tunnel paint two giant murals that will link to each other across the sea, and the catenary team head out in their van to look for repairs. Inside the tunnel, the high-speed trains are being slowed down by the air they push in front of them, and the only way to relieve the pressure is with tubes connecting one tunnel to another(n)

programme.title: Your Home Made Perfect
programme.desc: Architects Laura Jane Clark and Robert Jamison compete to win a commission to remodel a home in Teesside for couple Ruth and Lindsey. The couple have lived in their 1990s developer home in Middlesbrough for years, and now they have two young daughters, the property fails to meet the needs of their family(n)

programme.title: Catching Britain's Killers: The Crimes That Changed Us
programme.desc: The stories of murder investigations and their extraordinary consequences, which overturned laws, transformed police interrogation and revolutionised forensic detection. The second episode focuses on the story of the murder of Julie Hogg, a single mother from the small town of Billingham in Teesside. The programme looks back at Julie's mother Ann's campaign to challenge the 800-year-old law on Double Jeopardy, after a jury failed to convict Julie's former boyfriend Billy Dunlop. As this episode traces the twists and turns of Ann's campaign, the story of other cases that benefited from the legal changes are also explored(n)

programme.title: The Apprentice: You're Fired
programme.desc: The companion programme to The Apprentice, with Tom Allen talking to the third candidate to be ejected from the competition. The host is also joined by a panel of experts and fans, who offer an entertaining analysis of this week's task(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events. Presented by Emma Barnett(n)

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Barristers Jeremy Dein and Sasha Wass re-investigate an alleged false confession that led to the hanging of William Burtoft for the brutal murder of Frances Levin in her home in Manchester in 1933. Now, more than 80 years later, William's relative Kate is keen to learn more about the case and works alongside Sasha and Jeremy to uncover new details about her great-great-uncle. The case was based on a confession that William signed, but the barristers have grave concerns about how that confession was obtained - can they discover new evidence about the authenticity of the confession?(n)

programme.title: Back in Time for...
programme.desc: Part one of two. The Irwins experience 50 years of black British history, discovering how post-war Caribbean immigration transformed the way of life in the UK. The first edition sees them learn what life was like for arrivals coming to Britain in the 1950s and '60s, with presenter Giles Coren and social historian Emma Dabiri introducing them to their new homes and the family sampling the food, work and entertainment of first-generation West Indian immigrants in Brixton. With contributions by Kenny Lynch and Rudolph Walker(n)

programme.title: Back in Time for...
programme.desc: Part two of two. The Irwins experience life in the 1970s, '80s and '90s, discovering how a new generation of black Britons made their way through three decades of British history, guided by presenter Giles Coren and social historian Emma Dabiri. They meet former West Indies cricket captain Clive Lloyd and learn about the 1976 tour of England in which he led his side to victory, before reflecting on the Brixton Riots of the 1980s, and hosting a typical 1989-style party with DJ and producer Jazzie B of Soul II Soul(n)

programme.title: Marianne and Leonard: Words of Love (2019)
programme.desc: The lives of composer Leonard Cohen and his Norwegian muse Marianne Ihlen, whose romance began on the Greek island of Hydra in 1960 as part of a bohemian community of foreign artists, writers, and musicians. This film follows their relationship, from the early days on Hydra, a humble time of 'free love' and open marriage, to how their love evolved when Leonard became a successful musician. Along the way the film tells of the tragedy that befell those that could not survive the beauty of Hydra, the highs and lows of Leonard's career, and the inspirational power that Marianne possessed(n)

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Entrepreneurs Deborah Meaden, Peter Jones, Touker Suleyman, Tej Lalvani and Jenny Campbell assess more unusual business ideas, including a shocking display from the owner of a live anatomy dissection business and a new model of tuk-tuk that turns out to have a fatal flaw. The final presentation is for a singer-for-hire scheme, which prompts the panel to show off their own musical talents(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: EJ Osborne scours Llanfoist Recycling Centre for discarded items he can transform, including a piano stool and a photographer's lamp(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Perfect Town
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin offers advice to house hunters who want to move to the lively market town of Abergavenny in Monmouthshire(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team are at Lytham Hall in Lancashire, where items brought in include a rare Victorian automaton, a work by LS Lowry and medals won by Britain's first black Olympian. John Foster marvels at a briefcase full of artefacts from Ernest Shackleton's 1909 Antarctic expedition and Ronnie Morgan discovers a gift from a Russian tsar(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.desc: Jo Coburn and guests discuss the big political issues of the day.(n)

programme.title: The Boss
programme.desc: Susan Calman hosts the quiz in which one contestant is put in control and has to use judgement and tactics to decide which of the other players is best suited to answer(n)

programme.title: My Life on a Plate
programme.desc: Brian Turner accompanies astrologer Russell Grant as he visits places from his childhood, including the church where he was a choirboy and the flat where he lived with his parents(n)

programme.title: Emergency Rescue Down Under
programme.desc: A British pilot is scrambled to rescue a surfer who has been attacked by a shark and a Flying Doctor finds herself fighting to save a badly injured expat(n)

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: The probate detectives search for the relatives of a well-loved Leicestershire lady, and on another case they learn how the industrial revolution impacted Victorian bakeries(n)

programme.title: The Wonder of Animals
programme.desc: Chris Packham examines what lies beneath a crocodile's hard exterior, discovering the secret to its ancient history and gaining an insight into one of the most sophisticated physiologies on the planet. By following all stages of a crocodile hunt, Chris reveals how their extraordinary circulatory system enables them to hold their breath for more than an hour, and looks at how their exceptionally sensitive skin detects their prey through water(n)

programme.title: Earth's Greatest Spectacles
programme.desc: The north-eastern region of the United States, known as New England, is the stage for one of the most incredible colour changes on the planet when the vivid greens of summer give way to the golds and reds of autumn. This film reveals how this vibrant fiesta is created by the battles between the trees and the forests inhabitants. Moose, chipmunks, rattlesnakes and a bizarre mixture of caterpillars all play a crucial role; but surprisingly the forest itself was made to be so colourful, thanks to a combination of hard work by beavers, ants and humans. Narrated by Domhnall Gleeson(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Thomas Plant and David Fletcher head to Norwich Cathedral in Norfolk, where they unearth items of interest including an old astronomical map and a watercolour by a local artist(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Angellica Bell, Gyles Brandreth, Scarlett Moffatt and Iain Stirling test their knowledge in trivia-based games across the week, with the ultimate winner declared on Friday(n)

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Zoe Ball talks to some of the couples, chats to Ian Waite about the contestants' training progress and has a sneak peek at a few of Saturday's costumes with designer Vicky Gill(n)

programme.title: MOTDx
programme.desc: Jermaine Jenas is joined by Craig Mitch, Chelcee Grimes and Reece Parkinson to take an offbeat look at the Premier League, also discussing skills, celebrities, fashion, music and culture(n)

programme.title: The Channel Tunnel - Life on the Inside
programme.desc: Twenty-five years since the Channel Tunnel was opened, many staff face retirement and new recruits are being drafted in. But the tests for wannabe shuttle drivers are ruthless, with a 90 per cent fail rate. Meanwhile, supercars turn up in convoy for the Le Mans 24-hour race. But as the French step up their passport controls, and the screens break down at terminal control, there are concerns that the vehicles will not make it to France in time(n)

programme.title: Hairy Bikers: Route 66
programme.desc: Dave and Si cross the state of Arizona in search of the Wild West and make a small detour from Route 66, starting in Monument Valley, to learn more about this striking place. They're invited by a local Navajo police officer to eat with his family at a traditional Navajo barbecue, where a sheep is butchered, grilled and eaten alongside some traditional fry bread. Still riding in the Navajo Nation, the bikers stop at Tsaile College to learn more about rodeo and cook a lamb stew with corn dumplings in homage of the Navajo people they met(n)

programme.title: Giri/Haji
programme.desc: Tokyo detective Kenzo hears that a yakuza boss is being framed for a violent murder in the UK - and that the real culprit is his own brother, a man he thought was dead. Now his only choice is to travel to London in the hope of finding his brother before violence tears both cities apart. Crime thriller, starring Takehiro Hira and Kelly Macdonald(n)

programme.title: Later - with Jools Holland
programme.desc: The eponymous pianist returns for a new run, beginning with performances by five female artists making their debut on the show - hip-hop star Sampa the Great, Mercury Prize nominee Cate Le Bon, multi-instrumentalist Georgia, veteran soul singer PP Arnold and Arkansas singer Yebba. The latter performs with producer Mark Ronson, who co-hosts tomorrow's longer show with Jools tomorrow night(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events. Presented by Kirsty Wark(n)

programme.title: Catching Britain's Killers: The Crimes That Changed Us
programme.desc: The stories of murder investigations and their extraordinary consequences, which overturned laws, transformed police interrogation and revolutionised forensic detection. The second episode focuses on the story of the murder of Julie Hogg, a single mother from the small town of Billingham in Teesside. The programme looks back at Julie's mother Ann's campaign to challenge the 800-year-old law on Double Jeopardy, after a jury failed to convict Julie's former boyfriend Billy Dunlop. As this episode traces the twists and turns of Ann's campaign, the story of other cases that benefited from the legal changes are also explored(n)

programme.title: The Unwanted: The Secret Windrush Files
programme.desc: David Olusoga opens previously unseen government files to argue that the Windrush scandal and the so-called hostile environment for black British immigrants has been 70 years in the making. The film features Sarah O'Connor, Anthony Bryan and Judy Griffith. Settled in the UK legally since childhood, they were re-classified as illegal immigrants by new regulations. Unable to show proof of their nationality status, they lost jobs, savings and their health, facing deportation back to countries they could barely remember(n)

programme.title: Japan with Sue Perkins
programme.desc: Part one of two. Sue Perkins embarks upon a trip around Japan, exploring a nation caught between the demands of a hi-tech future and the pull of a traditional past. Sue begins her journey in the capital city of Tokyo, where she trains with a female sumo wrestling team, before visiting a family who live with robots. Then, she leaves the city behind to see the a different side of Japan, and explore its forests, mountains and islands(n)

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: Nick Knowles is in Lancashire, where a portrait artist, a textile-making farmer and a chainsaw sculptor compete to create artwork celebrating the buyers' love for their dogs(n)

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don assesses the progress, successes and failures of the paradise garden he planted at the start of the year. He also sets about sowing a meadow of wildflowers in the orchard and gives advice on what to plant now in the vegetable garden. Frances Tophill travels to Glasgow to meet a gardener whose hands-off approach to his garden has reaped rewards for wildlife, Adam Frost visits a garden in Wales where a potentially unpromising and rocky site has been transformed, while Mark Lane explores Nymans in West Sussex to find out how the garden is managed with sustainability at its heart(n)

programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Susan Calman in which one contestant becomes the Boss and has to use judgement and tactics to decide which player is best suited to answer questions(n)

programme.title: My Life on a Plate
programme.desc: As a child, Alistair McGowan loved school lunches, especially the puddings. His mother's cooking was equally memorable and innovative, providing plenty of inspiration for the dishes that Brian Turner creates for Alistair. Together they revisit evocative places from Alistair's childhood, from Worcestershire's plum orchards to his old school, where sights and smells take him straight back to his youth. In his childhood home, we discover how Alistair realised his gift for impersonation as he switches effortlessly into some of the voices that helped him make his name(n)

programme.title: Emergency Rescue Down Under
programme.desc: A British flying doctor fights to save the life of a biker in the back of a moving ambulance racing towards Sydney, while two UK policemen hunt a wanted suspect in the Outback(n)

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: A new lead opens up on a case that has been unsolved for 24 years - but will it be the clue the heir hunters have been waiting for? Meanwhile, the search for heirs of a Romford woman reveals a link to the story that inspired the film Made in Dagenham. Plus, details of two unclaimed estates where heirs still need to be found.(n)

programme.title: The Wonder of Animals
programme.desc: Chris Packham explores the hunting prowess of the most widespread of marine mammals - the dolphin. Contrary to its amiable reputation, it is in fact a ruthless predator, using a combination of specialised anatomy and complex communication to hunt. The programme also reveals how a pod employs body movements to disclose the location of food(n)

programme.title: Earth's Greatest Spectacles
programme.desc: Svalbard in the Arctic spends many months of the year in complete darkness, an unrelenting frozen winter with temperatures down to minus 40C. However, as this documentary reveals when the sun finally reappears, the landscape magically transforms from an ice world into a rich tundra, full of exotic plants, birds, arctic foxes, polar bears, walrus and reindeer. This transformation is only possible thanks to some bizarre micro-organisms that feed on ice and the stunning abilities of migrating birds. Narrated by Domhnall Gleeson(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: A collection of some of the best, unseen finds from the series, visiting the Milestones Museum in Basingstoke Ragley Hall in Warwickshire, the National Maritime Museum Cornwall in Falmouth, and the RAF museum in Hendon, London. Items include an art nouveau necklace and a pair of British railway lamps(n)

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Zoe Ball and Rylan Clark-Neal present analysis of the pro-celebrity couples' progress ahead of tomorrow night's show. Celebrity super fans discuss all things ballroom, while Gethin Jones catches up with the couples backstage at this week's Strictly rehearsals(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Angellica Bell, Gyles Brandreth, Scarlett Moffatt and Iain Stirling go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games, vying to be crowned weekly champion(n)

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys grills four more contenders on their chosen subjects, which are Middle Welsh tome The Mabinogion, the life of cellist Jacqueline du Pré, the life and works of fiction writer William Gibson, and socialite Viscountess Palmerston, aka Emily Temple. This is followed by a two-minute general knowledge round(n)

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don plants out berried shrubs to help feed wildlife in the winter, harvests pumpkins and shows how to make use of fallen autumnal leaves. Carol Klein shows the planting partners still making an impact in the borders of the Bishop's Palace and Gardens in Somerset, while Adam Frost is in the Cotswolds finding out about some surprising planting schemes designed with animals in mind. Frances Tophill summarises her first season on her allotment in Kent and Rachel de Thame updates her containers to ensure that they give interest for the upcoming seasons ahead(n)

programme.title: The Name of the Rose
programme.desc: The Abbot hears that the delegation led by Bernard Gui is on its way, prompting him to urge William to find the murderer before their arrival. William works with Severinus, the abbey's herbalist and scientist, to analyse the monk's body, concluding he was poisoned before thrown in the vat. They soon realise that a highly lethal poison from the herbalist's collection has been taken. When their trail leads them to the infamous library, William and Adso devise a plan to enter the labyrinth, but quickly realise not everything is as it seems once inside. John Turturro and Rupert Everett star(n)

programme.title: Amazing Hotels: Life Beyond the Lobby
programme.desc: Giles Coren and Monica Galetti travel to South America to work in Vira Vira, a hacienda hotel that overlooks an active volcano near Pucon, the adventure capital of Chile. Giles and Monica prepare a meal for guests, go trekking through the Chilean forests on horseback and hear how the chef has an abundance of Chilean produce for his recipes(n)

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig continues airing questions on the theme of Quests, this time inviting Susan Calman, Joe Lycett and Holly Walsh to join regular panellist Alan Davies. As usual, the questions are so difficult points are instead awarded for the answers the host finds most interesting as opposed to correct(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Kirsty Wark(n)

programme.title: Later - with Jools Holland
programme.desc: Extended edition of the music show. The veteran presenter's co-host for this edition discusses his love of working with new female voices, as well as picking his favourite clip from the Later vaults. The duo introduce performances by five female artists making their debut on the show - Arkansas singer Yebba, hip-hop star Sampa the Great, Mercury Prize nominee Cate Le Bon, multi-instrumentalist Georgia and veteran soul singer PP Arnold(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: Pubs, Ponds and Power: The Story of the Village
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson explores the history of Cromford in Derbyshire, which was at the heart of 18th-century industrialist Richard Arkwright's mechanised cotton mills and a textile revolution. Ben discovers Cromford became a new kind of village, built to service the enterprise of this powerful man(n)

programme.title: Castles: Britain's Fortified History
programme.desc: Historian Sam Willis charts how Edward I turned the castle into an instrument of colonisation. He spent a large amount of money on subduing the Welsh and built fearsome fortresses in Caernarfon, Gwynedd and Beaumaris, which were used to impose England's rule on the country. However, when the king's attention turned to Scotland, his plans didn't go as expected(n)

programme.title: Hollywood's Brightest Bombshell: The Hedy Lamarr Story (2017)
programme.desc: The life and career of the actress, who fled from her Nazi-sympathiser husband Friedrich Mandl in Austria and became a Hollywood star. Narrated by Diane Kruger(n)

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: Professor Alice Roberts investigates how science came to understand sex - a simple word for a very complex set of desires, including individual passions, wants and emotions. In this documentary, she trawls through 45 years of Horizon archive to examine how the world of science has strived to solve people's sexual problems, and searched for ways of improving performance(n)

programme.title: Sex, Lies and Love Bites: The Agony Aunt Story
programme.desc: Philippa Perry explores the enduring appeal of the problem page, as she unpicks three centuries of advice on broken hearts, cheating partners and adolescent angst to uncover more about Britain's social history. The presenter talks to fellow agony aunt and uncle Deidre Sanders and Graham Norton about their experiences, and also looks at the advice of columnists of the past, such as the 17th century inventor of the genre, John Dunton, to see why the same problems are as prevalent now as they were hundreds of years ago(n)

programme.title: Timeshift
programme.desc: Babita Sharma explores the growing and shifting fortunes of corner shops to discover why this unsung hero has been at the centre of ordinary lives for more than 70 years. Featuring contributions by comedian Sanjeev Singh Kholi and actor Nitin Ganatra, Babita uses the shop as a way to explore the social fabric of Britain - from economic change to immigration(n)

programme.title: Two Types: The Faces of Britain
programme.desc: Mark Ovenden tells the stories of Johnston and Gill Sans, the two modern typefaces that have come to be regarded as the 'faces' of Britain(n)

programme.title: Hollywood's Brightest Bombshell: The Hedy Lamarr Story (2017)
programme.desc: The life and career of the actress, who fled from her Nazi-sympathiser husband Friedrich Mandl in Austria and became a Hollywood star. Narrated by Diane Kruger(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1988
programme.desc: Liz Kershaw and Bruno Brookes present the edition that first aired on August 11, with Mica Paris featuring Courtney Pine, All About Eve, Breathe, Iron Maiden, Tanita Tikaram, Brother Beyond, Yazz and the Plastic Population and BVSMP(n)

programme.title: Forces of Nature with Brian Cox
programme.desc: The physicist charts the Earth's journey through space, taking to the air in a fighter jet to race the spin of the planet and reverse the passage of the day. In Brazil, a professional surfer rides a monstrous wave surges up the Amazon River, marking the world's constant dance with the Moon, while the harsh winter in Greenland brings opportunity to the Inuit people who live there, and Brian explains how comprehending the motion and speed of Earth brings an understanding of the nature of space and time itself(n)

programme.title: Own the Sky: Jet Pack Dreamers
programme.desc: The story of Australian David Mayman, whose passion for the tantalising possibilities of jetpacks became an obsession. Filmed over 10 years, the documentary follows his seemingly impossible quest to realise his childhood dream to build and fly the world's first jetpack - an ambition that almost cost him his life(n)

programme.title: How to Build
programme.desc: Documentary following workers at Rolls-Royce's Derby factory as they construct the company's Trent 1000 jet engine in preparation for its maiden flight attached to a Boeing 787 Dreamliner. The film explores the technology involved in its construction, and charts the ups and downs of working on the assembly line. Narrated by Gerard Fletcher(n)

programme.title: Pain, Pus & Poison: The Search for Modern Medicines
programme.desc: Documentary exploring how scientists exploited the natural world to make medicines, beginning by investigating the challenges they faced trying to create a drug that would alleviate pain. The programme examines what physical discomfort actually is, why people want to control it, and how the discovery of morphine led to 200 years of scientific breakthroughs and self-experimentation(n)

programme.title: Francesco's Venice
programme.desc: Francesco da Mosto charts the changes that took place as Venice became increasingly prosperous, marking the city's great age of trading, while artists such as Titian, Veronese and Tintoretto heralded a golden era of Venetian painting. Merchant princes began building resplendent palaces along the Grand Canal, only for disaster to strike in 1575 as plague hit the city, killing almost a third of the population(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: In the wake of the First World War, former sapper Thomas Shelby and his brothers return from the trenches to their hometown of Birmingham, where their family runs a feared crime ring - making money from illegal betting, protection and the black market. Thomas recognises an opportunity to move up in the world when a crate of guns goes missing from an arms factory, but government minister Winston Churchill sends in a ruthless police chief from Belfast to impose order on the increasingly lawless city and recover the weapons. First episode of the drama, starring Cillian Murphy and Sam Neill(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: Chief Inspector Campbell carries out a violent raid on the gang's territory and demands a meeting with Thomas, who is later sent a bullet with his name on it after picking a fight with a gypsy family at a country fair. Meanwhile, Aunt Polly is shocked to discover Ada is pregnant, and when Thomas discovers his former war comrade Freddie Thorne is the father, he devises a plan to get his old friend out of town. Drama, starring Cillian Murphy(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: Two men claiming to be IRA sympathisers approach Thomas in the Garrison and offer to buy the missing crate of guns they have heard he has in his possession. The gang leader also goes to Cheltenham races with Grace, where he aims to persuade kingpin Billy Kimber to make the Peaky Blinders part of his operation, while Ada secretly marries communist agitator Freddie Thorne. Drama, starring Cillian Murphy(n)

programme.title: World News Today
programme.desc: The day's leading stories(n)

programme.title: The Live Lounge Show
programme.desc: Clara Amfo introduces a performance by English singer-songwriter Charlotte Aitchison, aka Charli XCX, who began her performing career posting songs to social media. American DJ, singer and songwriter musician Mark Ronson and US rapper and songwriter Anderson Paak also feature(n)

programme.title: Rock 'n' Roll America
programme.desc: The mid-1950s saw Elvis Presley make his TV debut with Heartbreak Hotel, following it with a gyrating version of Hound Dog that outraged the conservative media. A 'cleaned-up' artist was required, and Christian family man Pat Boone sated that need. While Jerry Lee Lewis caused upset with Whole Lotta Shakin and the Everlys shocked with Wake Up Little Susie, bespectacled geek Buddy Holly calmed things down with his school friends the Crickets. Contributors include Jerry Lee Lewis, Don Everly and Tom Jones(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1988
programme.desc: Mike Read and Simon Mayo present the edition that first aired on August 18, featuring performances by Aztec Camera, Kylie Minogue, Chris Rea, Status Quo, Van Halen, Big Country, Fairground Attraction, Julio Iglesias and Stevie Wonder, Yazz and the Plastic Population, and Robbie Robertson(n)

programme.title: Eric Clapton: Life in 12 Bars
programme.desc: Documentary profiling the blues guitarist, narrated by the man himself, as he charts his six decades in the music business, and why his insatiable desire to grow his artistic voice led him to quit a stream of successful bands, from the Yardbirds and John Mayall and the Bluesbreakers to short-lived supergroups Cream and Blind Faith. Clapton also talks about his battles with drugs and alcohol, his love for George Harrison's wife Pattie Boyd and the tragic loss of his son in an accident. Including archive interviews with BB King, George Harrison and Jimi Hendrix(n)

programme.title: Eric Clapton at the BBC - The Rock 'n' Roll Years
programme.desc: Archive performances by the guitarist and songwriter, who has survived both personal trauma and drug abuse to build a wide and varied musical career. He began with R&B group the Yardbirds, before moving on to join John Mayall and the Bluesbreakers. He also collaborated with Ginger Baker and Jack Bruce in Cream, and worked with Steve Winwood and John Lennon. He now enjoys success as a solo performer, and is often acclaimed as one of the greatest guitarists of his generation(n)

Expert rock movers Bevel, Zip and Pop arrive, but can't move it either. Looking for things to help them, Bitz finds some logs to roll the rock, but Bevel stops to eat a banana and the rock rolls off out of control, causing the logs to go everywhere. programme.title: The Truth About
programme.desc: Doctor Javid Abdelmoneim explores the science behind drinking alcohol, including why the weekly recommended intake was lowered in January, with the men's limit cut by a third to 14 units per week. He also asks whether a nightcap can aid sleep, why some people get drunk quicker than others, what is behind the relatively healthy reputation of red wine and whether lining your stomach before drinking really lessens the effects of a night out. Plus, he assesses new evidence for the health risks posed by alcohol(n)

programme.title: Watchdog
programme.desc: Matt Allwright, Steph McGovern and Nikki Fox present the consumer investigation show, including a confrontation with a tech repair business that instead of fixing faults creates new ones - and even holds customers' gadgets hostage. Anneka Rice investigates whether carbon offsetting can really mean guilt-free-flying, and the team ranks the best or worst airlines(n)

programme.title: The Met: Policing London
programme.desc: The observational documentary about the Metropolitan Police continues with the ongoing problem of moped crime. A young woman is rushed to hospital after being violently mugged by two moped-riding robbers who took her phone and handbag. The detectives get a lead just 15 minutes later when two men use the victim's bank card. But tracking them proves more difficult. The film also follows the Met officers keeping crowds safe at the Notting Hill Carnival, at a time when knife crime in the capital has become a major concern(n)

programme.title: Question Time

programme.title: Brexitcast

programme.desc: Naga Munchetty and Charlie Stayt present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

Plus, the cost of watching television in hospital, and whether excessive profiteering is going on(n)

programme.title: Gareth Edwards' Great Welsh Adventure

The duo take charge of a narrowboat on the Llangollen Canal, but Gareth's stress levels go soon through the roof as he tries to avoid hitting obstacles. Somehow, Wales' much-loved couple still find time to appreciate the breath-taking scenery(n)

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: David Jason returns as Granville in this follow-up to Roy Clarke's popular comedy Open All Hours, as he re-opens the doors of the nation's favourite corner shop. In the opening episode, Arkwright's protege finds an old mangle in the yard - and wonders how he can best put it to use. Meanwhile, Beth continues to pressure Leroy to find a date for her best friend, Ruby, who has been suspended at work. Elsewhere, Eric and Cyril tutor Mr Newbold in the art of romance - and how to get closer to Mrs Rossi. Co-starring James Baxter, Johnny Vegas and Katie Redford(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Sonia is alarmed when she sees Martin's injuries and when he refuses to seek medical help, she grows increasingly agitated with his lack of consideration for Bex. Stuart visits Kathy and makes his feelings plain, while Whitney is adamant that she and Leo stay in the E20 when Callum and Ben walk in - but she proceeds to get drunk. programme.title: The Truth About
programme.desc: Doctor Javid Abdelmoneim explores the science behind drinking alcohol, including why the weekly recommended intake was lowered in January, with the men's limit cut by a third to 14 units per week. He also asks whether a nightcap can aid sleep, why some people get drunk quicker than others, what is behind the relatively healthy reputation of red wine and whether lining your stomach before drinking really lessens the effects of a night out. Plus, he assesses new evidence for the health risks posed by alcohol(n)

programme.title: The Met: Policing London
programme.desc: The observational documentary about the Metropolitan Police continues with the ongoing problem of moped crime. The detectives get a lead just 15 minutes later when two men use the victim's bank card. But tracking them proves more difficult. The film also follows the Met officers keeping crowds safe at the Notting Hill Carnival, at a time when knife crime in the capital has become a major concern(n)

programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. programme.title: Heathrow: Britain's Busiest Airport
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes of the airport, finding out how its infrastructure helps staff process thousands of passengers arriving from 84 countries. programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Chesney takes Gemma to the hospital after she is finally set free from the turnstile, and Bethany asks James about his life as a professional footballer. Marion reveals to Shona that she is Max's grandma and also claims that Shona is an unfit mother(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Drama series, starring Martin Clunes as a surgeon who is forced to retrain as a GP and takes up a post in a sleepy Cornish village where he soon finds himself at odds with the locals. This time, Martin has his second GMC assessment - mock surgery - and Buddy is missing. Meanwhile, Ken's arrival does not bring good news for Al and Bert, and Penhale unhappy with the arrival of his predecessor, Mark Mylow, who tries to take charge of his investigation. Starring Martin Clunes, Caroline Catz and Ian McNeice(n)

programme.title: Peston

programme.title: Alexander Armstrong's Land of the Midnight Sun
programme.desc: The presenter continues his epic 8,000-mile journey halfway around the Arctic Circle deep in mid-winter. He embarks on the second leg of his odyssey, beginning in Iceland before travelling to the icy wilderness of Greenland. Along the way, Alexander snorkels over a vast underwater crevasse separating two continental landmasses and wrestles with a female Viking. He also joins two Danish Special Forces soldiers and their huskies, who have the loneliest job on the planet, and witnesses an array of natural wonders(n)

programme.title: Tonight
programme.desc: With £7billion already spent on Britain's controversial north-south high-speed rail link, a government review is now considering whether or not to advise ministers to abandon the project. HS2 promised to slash rail travel times, create capacity and join up the nation, but it's already over-budget, running late and, as yet, not an inch of track has been laid(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Aaron and Robert reminisce, Graham opens up to Rhona, and Chas sobs on Paddy's shoulder(n)

programme.title: Paul O'Grady's Little Heroes
programme.desc: Paul meets charming four-year-old Lucy, who has travelled down from Glasgow for the latest check-up to monitor her pulmonary hypertension - a high blood pressure condition that affects the lungs. He also meets remarkable 13-year-old Connor, who is on the isolation ward as he prepares for a bone marrow transplant to help treat leukaemia. As Connor needs to live in a totally sterile environment to protect his weakened immune system, Paul can only speak to him through a window - but that doesn't stop him finding a number of fun and interesting ways to keep Connor occupied during his recovery(n)

programme.title: Inside Prison: Britain Behind Bars
programme.desc: The reality of life inside one Britain's 30 Young Offenders Institutions - HMYOI Deerbolt in County Durham. Following weeks of violence and unrest, caused by the influx of drugs and mobile phone being smuggled in, a clampdown has been put in force. Acting on intelligence that inmates have been targeting the weekly Muslim prayer service to exchange illicit items, officers, along with the Imam Muhammad Meah, face a battle to regain control. Elsewhere at HMP Bullingdon, officers in the Segregation Unit are dealing with a prisoner on a Dirty Protest who refuses to leave the wing and attempt to appease the situation with newly recruited prison officer Beth Buchanan(n)

programme.title: The Late Debate

programme.title: The Jonathan Ross Show

programme.title: Sanditon
programme.desc: It's the final visit to the Sunday night period drama, giving viewers a chance to find out how writer Andrew Davies has brought his adaptation of Jane Austen's unfinished novel to an end. On the night of the Midsummer Ball, romantic interests are finally resolved. However, the fate of Sanditon is in the balance when events conspire to leave Charlotte and the Parker family facing down impossible choices. Rose Williams stars as the feisty heroine, with Theo James, Anne Reid and Kris Marshall(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Drama series, starring Martin Clunes as a surgeon who is forced to retrain as a GP and takes up a post in a sleepy Cornish village where he soon finds himself at odds with the locals. programme.title: Alexander Armstrong's Land of the Midnight Sun
programme.desc: The presenter continues his epic 8,000-mile journey halfway around the Arctic Circle deep in mid-winter. He embarks on the second leg of his odyssey, beginning in Iceland before travelling to the icy wilderness of Greenland. Along the way, Alexander snorkels over a vast underwater crevasse separating two continental landmasses and wrestles with a female Viking. He also joins two Danish Special Forces soldiers and their huskies, who have the loneliest job on the planet, and witnesses an array of natural wonders(n)

programme.desc: With £7billion already spent on Britain's controversial north-south high-speed rail link, a government review is now considering whether or not to advise ministers to abandon the project. programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Aaron and Robert reminisce, Graham opens up to Rhona, and Chas sobs on Paddy's shoulder(n)

programme.title: Paul O'Grady's Little Heroes
programme.desc: Paul meets charming four-year-old Lucy, who has travelled down from Glasgow for the latest check-up to monitor her pulmonary hypertension - a high blood pressure condition that affects the lungs. He also meets remarkable 13-year-old Connor, who is on the isolation ward as he prepares for a bone marrow transplant to help treat leukaemia. As Connor needs to live in a totally sterile environment to protect his weakened immune system, Paul can only speak to him through a window - but that doesn't stop him finding a number of fun and interesting ways to keep Connor occupied during his recovery(n)

programme.title: Inside Prison: Britain Behind Bars
programme.desc: The reality of life inside one Britain's 30 Young Offenders Institutions - HMYOI Deerbolt in County Durham. Following weeks of violence and unrest, caused by the influx of drugs and mobile phone being smuggled in, a clampdown has been put in force. Acting on intelligence that inmates have been targeting the weekly Muslim prayer service to exchange illicit items, officers, along with the Imam Muhammad Meah, face a battle to regain control. Elsewhere at HMP Bullingdon, officers in the Segregation Unit are dealing with a prisoner on a Dirty Protest who refuses to leave the wing and attempt to appease the situation with newly recruited prison officer Beth Buchanan(n)

programme.title: The Late Debate

programme.title: The Jonathan Ross Show

programme.desc: It's the final visit to the Sunday night period drama, giving viewers a chance to find out how writer Andrew Davies has brought his adaptation of Jane Austen's unfinished novel to an end. As he sets out to steal the technology she needs to unleash chaos on the world, he is drawn into conflict with both old enemies and his former gang. Action adventure sequel, starring Vin Diesel, Charlize Theron, Dwayne Johnson and Kurt Russell(n)

programme.title: FYI Daily
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest entertainment news(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Merton organises a search of the moors after a farming couple are reported missing, leaving Jenny and Liz to look after their bemused children. Rural drama, starring Geoffrey Hughes, Duncan Bell and Sarah Tansey(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Stan and Jack decide to get fit, disturbed to learn that bus crews have a short life expectancy. Comedy, with Reg Varney(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Festive episode of the 1970s sitcom about a chirpy driver and his conductor pal who see it as their duty to run rings around the long-suffering inspector of the Luxton Bus Company. Stan and Jack look forward to a welcome seasonal break, but on Christmas Eve a workmate phones in sick and the petulant pair are rostered to provide cover for him(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Robin comes home with a puppy, to Chrissy's dismay. Flat-share comedy, with Richard O'Sullivan, Sally Thomsett and Paula Wilcox(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: An archaeologist dies of a heart attack shortly after smashing the seal of an ancient Egyptian tomb, raising the spectre of the mummy's curse. The grieving widow summons the Belgian sleuth to unravel the mystery, but he can only smoke out the real villain by risking his own life. David Suchet stars, with Anna Cropper and Bill Bailey(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A dentist falls in love with his young assistant. His anxious wife, believing he has made plans to poison her and thereby remove the main obstacle to happiness, calls for Poirot's help. Drama, starring David Suchet(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Lord Ashfordly's sister arrives in Aidensfield with a renowned Polish violinist and his daughter, intending to stage a concert at the village hall. Disaster strikes when an expensive instrument is stolen - and romance blossoms for Steve as he steps in to investigate the theft. Meanwhile, a shady father-and-son duo set their scheming sights on Vernon. Drama, starring James Carlton and Geoffrey Hughes(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Amos is unsure of Annie's motives, while Lynn has suspicions about Michael. A photograph gives Alan a turn, and Frank faces a proposal(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Emotions run high in Beckindale, Annie lets go, Chris goes for Lynn, and Zoe feels she's gone too far(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Maud sets herself up as a palm reader in Bet's living room. Vera is told to expect good fortune, while Maud reveals to Curly that he's already met his future wife(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Raquel refuses to work at the pub. Andy books the registry office. Curly wonders if Elaine is his future wife and unnerves her by following her around(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The bell-ringers of Midsomer Wellow are mysteriously murdered one by one - and it seems an ancient rhyme may hold the key to the killer's motive. As Barnaby and Troy embark on a baffling investigation in an attempt to determine the truth, Joyce starts digging into the past to discover the assassin's motives. John Nettles, Daniel Casey and Hugh Bonneville star(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Lord Ashfordly's sister arrives in Aidensfield with a renowned Polish violinist and his daughter, intending to stage a concert at the village hall. Disaster strikes when an expensive instrument is stolen - and romance blossoms for Steve as he steps in to investigate the theft. Meanwhile, a shady father-and-son duo set their scheming sights on Vernon. Drama, starring James Carlton and Geoffrey Hughes(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A wealthy banker goes missing while walking from his country house to the village post office. Poirot, who has acquired a parrot and developed a taste for conjuring tricks, bets the baffled Inspector Japp £5 he can solve the mystery without leaving his own apartment. David Suchet stars(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The dapper Belgian sleuth goes on an Egyptian cruise on board an old steamer, but when an American heiress is murdered, his little grey cells go into overdrive. On questioning each of his fellow passengers, he realises many of them have secrets they want to remain hidden. David Suchet stars, with James Fox, Judy Parfitt, Emily Blunt, Daisy Donovan, Frances de la Tour and David Soul(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: An American businessman fears his life is in danger while travelling on the Orient Express from Istanbul to Paris, and offers fellow passenger Poirot $10,000 for protection. After rejecting the proposal, the sleuth wakes up the following morning to learn the man has been stabbed to death while the train was held up by heavy snow in Serbia. Racing against time to find the killer among the passengers, Poirot must look to the past to solve the crime, and is faced with a decision that will haunt him for ever. David Suchet takes the title role in the murder mystery, with guest stars Hugh Bonneville, David Morrissey and Eileen Atkins(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Brooks and Devlin investigate when former top-flight footballer Robbie Nichols is murdered in the East End of London, in what appears to be a robbery gone wrong. They question a man seen in the area on the night of the killing, who protests his innocence and points the finger at a known gangster he claims was also at the scene. With the detectives at odds over who to believe, Steel finds it increasingly difficult to prosecute the accused. Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber and Ben Daniels star(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: High-court judge Rachel Callaghan (guest star Juliet Stevenson) is critically injured after being shot in the underground car park of her apartment. When it becomes clear a hitman was responsible, the team tries to discover who hired him. As evidence mounts against the accused, the judge's health deteriorates, and Castle is faced with a choice between friendship and respect for a person's right to die. Bill Paterson, Bradley Walsh and Freema Agyeman star(n)

Vintage comedy, starring Reg Varney, Anna Karen, Michael Robbins and Stephen Lewis(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Stan decides to re-upholster some chairs, smuggling some material used for the bus seats out of the depot. However, Inspector Blake unexpectedly calls in at his home, resulting in the need to rapidly camoflage the furniture. Vintage comedy, starring Reg Varney and Stephen Lewis(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Mr Roper decides he will not allow the flatmates to have a party. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Wealthy industrialist Sir Reuben Astwell is murdered after making a deal to sell synthetic rubber to the Germans, leaving the dapper sleuth wondering whether the motive was commercial or political. Evidence found in his chemical factory could hold the key to the mystery - but Poirot is unsure if the victim's widow can be trusted. Starring David Suchet, with Ann Bell and Bill Wallis(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A wealthy banker goes missing while walking from his country house to the village post office. Poirot, who has acquired a parrot and developed a taste for conjuring tricks, bets the baffled Inspector Japp £5 he can solve the mystery without leaving his own apartment. David Suchet stars(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Sgt Merton finds himself under pressure when teenagers commit serious offences in the area and must race to stop a planned 'chicken run' with stolen cars. The youngsters are also suspected of drug dealing after Liz Merrick hands in amphetamines found in a local boy's bedroom, and Merton's investigative techniques lead Jenny to call off the wedding. Starring Duncan Bell, Sarah Tansey and Aislin McGuckin(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: There's discord in the village. Joe has been neglected, Elizabeth is taken for granted and Zoe finds herself misunderstood(n)

programme.desc: Nick is anxious about Archie, and Kathy makes a very worrying discovery(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jack tries to get Vera to put her money on an accumulator. Curly refuses to let Andy and Amy have a day off together. The Platts throw a party(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Raquel is thrilled to be invited to sign up for an advanced modelling course. Liz is horrified when Andy invites her to his wedding. Vera wins big at bingo(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A pupil at the prestigious Devington School dies of a stab wound while competing in a race in honour of his recently deceased grandfather. DCI Barnaby discovers the death could be linked to a long-running rivalry between the school and the locals - or to the youngster's membership of an elite club dedicated to dining on suet puddings. Mystery, starring John Nettles, Daniel Casey and Jane Wymark(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Sgt Merton finds himself under pressure when teenagers commit serious offences in the area and must race to stop a planned 'chicken run' with stolen cars. The youngsters are also suspected of drug dealing after Liz Merrick hands in amphetamines found in a local boy's bedroom, and Merton's investigative techniques lead Jenny to call off the wedding. Starring Duncan Bell, Sarah Tansey and Aislin McGuckin(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian sleuth contemplates retiring while on a trip to the Lake District, but shelves his plans when Hastings meets a woman whose collection of valuable miniature portraits has been stolen. David Suchet stars(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Hathaway and Lewis investigate the murder of a businesswoman connected with an organisation that supports gifted children, who had apparently been blackmailing the father of one particularly promising youngster. Further deaths follow, and as the body count rises, they realise there is a decades-old secret at the heart of the case. Matters are complicated still further by the presence in Oxford of former MI5 spymaster Grace Orde, who is visiting to sign copies of her tell-all memoir - which provides a vital clue to the killer's identity. Detective drama, guest starring Anna Chancellor and Cherie Lunghi, with Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: A pregnant junior doctor is found beaten to death in a hospital car park, and her boyfriend Joe Nash becomes the prime suspect when he cannot provide an alibi. Brooks and Devlin begin to think Nash is having an affair with his therapist, but what initially seems like a crime of passion unravels into a conspiracy involving senior government officials, with Steel and Phillips risking their careers to find the truth. Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber and Matthew McNulty star(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: When a teenager with severe learning difficulties is killed in a fire at his home, Brooks and Devlin's investigation leads them to the last person they thought could be responsible. Matters are further complicated when Steel and Phillips come up against brash defence lawyer Dominic Peck, whose win-at-all-costs attitude and outrageous ego threaten the fair outcome of the trial. Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber and Ben Daniels star(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: A former bookie falls under suspicion for the murder of a hospital benefactor, who is found dead in his car. As the detectives investigate the case, it becomes clear their prime suspect had a strong motive, believing the victim was responsible for putting his daughter into a coma. Brian Cox guest stars, with John Thaw and Kevin Whately(n)

programme.desc: Stan faces a cash crisis so his mother lets out the front room to Luxton & District buses' new transport manager(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: A series of complaints prompts Inspector Blake to devise a new exit and entry system for the buses to leave and return to the depot. However, Stan falls foul of the new guidelines. Reg Varney and Stephen Lewis star(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Chrissy and Jo try to foil a showdown at the Mucky Duck Saloon when Robin and loud Irishman Mick get into an argument. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A mysterious delivery causes Poirot to recall the time his supper at a Buenos Aires restaurant was rudely interrupted by the murder of a fellow diner, who unwittingly drank from a glass laced with cyanide. Drama, starring David Suchet and Philip Jackson(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian sleuth contemplates retiring while on a trip to the Lake District, but shelves his plans when Hastings meets a woman whose collection of valuable miniature portraits has been stolen. David Suchet stars(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: PC Steve Crane takes an urgent phone call, and is shocked to discover Peter Pyke's warehouse has gone up in smoke. It soon emerges that the owner's wife had spent time behind bars for fraud, and her husband is reluctant to answer questions - leading Merton to suspect the blaze was deliberate. Meanwhile, Vernon moves into his new mansion, but has trouble keeping up with his well-to-do neighbours. Rural drama, starring James Carlton, Duncan Bell and Geoffrey Hughes(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: A rejection forces Turner to reconsider. Pollard considers an opportunity. Zoe comes out with some news and there's an unwelcome face in The Woolpack(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Turner's attempt to save face is ill-advised, while Jack's advice gives Leonard food for thought. Elizabeth and Pollard have an unpleasant encounter(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Kevin receives a date for his court case. Andy receives a shock when he goes to meet Amy at the airport. Vera announces she's spending her winnings on Joss's funeral(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Liz is delighted to hear Amy isn't coming back. Steve looks after Andy when he gets drunk. Curly is surprised when Elaine agrees to have dinner with him(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A prominent resident is robbed and battered to death with her walking stick, and suspicion soon falls on the members of her reading club - which turns out to be a front for a lucrative investment portfolio. John Nettles and Daniel Casey star, with guest appearances by Anton Rodgers (Fresh Fields) and Angela Thorne (To the Manor Born)(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: PC Steve Crane takes an urgent phone call, and is shocked to discover Peter Pyke's warehouse has gone up in smoke. programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: An FBI agent crosses swords with Poirot while trying to retrieve the American Navy's top-secret plans for a new submarine, which have been stolen by a glamorous nightclub singer with a sideline in espionage. Drama, starring David Suchet(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuth is called to Gossington Hall by her old friend Dolly Bantry (Joanna Lumley) when she finds the corpse of an unknown blonde in the library of her home. It soon transpires that there is no shortage of suspects, including local bad boy Basil Blake (Ben Miller) and Dolly's husband Arthur (James Fox), but when the victim is identified as a dancer in line to inherit a fortune, enquiries take a different turn. Crime drama, starring Geraldine McEwan, with Simon Callow, Jack Davenport and Tara Fitzgerald(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: A crackdown on an organised crime racket leads Foyle and Milner to a shipyard owned by the Talbot brothers, a duo with dubious links to a communist agitator who has recently moved to the area. When an engineer is murdered just hours after working at the shipyard, the detectives desperately try to track down the killer and discover the truth surrounding the Talbots' shady business dealings. Peter Capaldi guest stars in the wartime drama, with Michael Kitchen, Anthony Howell and Honeysuckle Weeks(n)

The youngsters are also suspected of drug dealing after Liz Merrick hands in amphetamines found in a local boy's bedroom, and Merton's investigative techniques lead Jenny to call off the wedding. Starring Duncan Bell, Sarah Tansey and Aislin McGuckin(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: There's discord in the village. Joe has been neglected, Elizabeth is taken for granted and Zoe finds herself misunderstood(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Nick is anxious about Archie, and Kathy makes a very worrying discovery(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jack tries to get Vera to put her money on an accumulator. Curly refuses to let Andy and Amy have a day off together. The Platts throw a party(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Raquel is thrilled to be invited to sign up for an advanced modelling course. Liz is horrified when Andy invites her to his wedding. Vera wins big at bingo(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A pupil at the prestigious Devington School dies of a stab wound while competing in a race in honour of his recently deceased grandfather. DCI Barnaby discovers the death could be linked to a long-running rivalry between the school and the locals - or to the youngster's membership of an elite club dedicated to dining on suet puddings. Mystery, starring John Nettles, Daniel Casey and Jane Wymark(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Sgt Merton finds himself under pressure when teenagers commit serious offences in the area and must race to stop a planned 'chicken run' with stolen cars. The youngsters are also suspected of drug dealing after Liz Merrick hands in amphetamines found in a local boy's bedroom, and Merton's investigative techniques lead Jenny to call off the wedding. Starring Duncan Bell, Sarah Tansey and Aislin McGuckin(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian sleuth contemplates retiring while on a trip to the Lake District, but shelves his plans when Hastings meets a woman whose collection of valuable miniature portraits has been stolen. David Suchet stars(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Hathaway and Lewis investigate the murder of a businesswoman connected with an organisation that supports gifted children, who had apparently been blackmailing the father of one particularly promising youngster. Further deaths follow, and as the body count rises, they realise there is a decades-old secret at the heart of the case. Matters are complicated still further by the presence in Oxford of former MI5 spymaster Grace Orde, who is visiting to sign copies of her tell-all memoir - which provides a vital clue to the killer's identity. Detective drama, guest starring Anna Chancellor and Cherie Lunghi, with Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: A pregnant junior doctor is found beaten to death in a hospital car park, and her boyfriend Joe Nash becomes the prime suspect when he cannot provide an alibi. Brooks and Devlin begin to think Nash is having an affair with his therapist, but what initially seems like a crime of passion unravels into a conspiracy involving senior government officials, with Steel and Phillips risking their careers to find the truth. Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber and Matthew McNulty star(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: When a teenager with severe learning difficulties is killed in a fire at his home, Brooks and Devlin's investigation leads them to the last person they thought could be responsible. Matters are further complicated when Steel and Phillips come up against brash defence lawyer Dominic Peck, whose win-at-all-costs attitude and outrageous ego threaten the fair outcome of the trial. Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber and Ben Daniels star(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: A former bookie falls under suspicion for the murder of a hospital benefactor, who is found dead in his car. As the detectives investigate the case, it becomes clear their prime suspect had a strong motive, believing the victim was responsible for putting his daughter into a coma. Brian Cox guest stars, with John Thaw and Kevin Whately(n)
